workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed this workflow's workflow.yaml
Communicate all responses in {communication_language} tailored to {user_skill_level}
Default to an accept-default experience that gets the workspace ready for next-step with minimal user friction.
If td has no open issues, that is NOT completion for initialize. You must bootstrap BMAD planning and td tasks before finishing.

step.goal: Initialize td session and inspect workspace state
step.action: Run `td usage --new-session` to initialize td context
Inspect whether BMAD planning artifacts exist (PRD, architecture, epics, stories)
Inspect td state (existing issues, parent-child links, dependency links, reviewable/ready counts)
Classify project mode as greenfield or brownfield automatically unless user explicitly overrides
Build a readiness snapshot with booleans: has_prd, has_product_brief, has_architecture, has_epics_or_stories, has_td_epic, has_td_tasks
Treat "no open issues" or `td next` empty output as a setup signal, not a stop signal

check.action: Onboard existing project into BMAD by creating or updating recommended artifacts needed for execution flow

check.if: has_prd or has_product_brief, but has_architecture is false
check.action: Execute `_bmad/bmm/workflows/3-solutioning/create-architecture/workflow.md` directly via `_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml` using defaults (do not rely on slash command availability)
Execute `_bmad/bmm/workflows/3-solutioning/create-epics-and-stories/workflow.md` directly via `_bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml` using defaults (do not rely on slash command availability)
Map BMAD stories/tasks to td issues with explicit dependency links
Backfill missing dependency metadata for existing td issues where possible
Prefer deterministic defaults and avoid unnecessary prompts
Run BMAD initialization workflow and create foundational planning artifacts
Select recommended BMAD workflows based on project scope and complexity defaults

Ensure planning outputs include architecture plus epics/stories by executing workflow files directly (`_bmad/bmm/workflows/3-solutioning/create-architecture/workflow.md` and `_bmad/bmm/workflows/3-solutioning/create-epics-and-stories/workflow.md`) when missing
Create td epics/tasks from the selected workflow outputs with dependency links mapped
Prefer deterministic defaults and avoid unnecessary prompts

check.if: has_td_epic is false or has_td_tasks is false after onboarding
check.action: Create td epics and child tasks from BMAD stories now; do not exit initialize without actionable td structure

step.action: Re-check td state after onboarding and confirm at least one of: reviewable issues, ready issues, or a defined epic-level next action
Verify td graph has actionable structure (reviewable, ready, or epic-level next action)
Verify BMAD artifacts and td metadata are synchronized enough for autonomous execution
If critical setup gaps remain, fix them in-place using defaults

check.if: td still has no epic and no tasks
check.action: Run BMAD planning bootstrap by executing workflow files directly (`create-architecture` then `create-epics-and-stories`) and regenerate td mapping
Create a td task: "Set up validation methodology" and tag as bmad
Set dependency so review-heavy work references this setup task before final approvals when possible
Record recommendation to run `/bmad:td:setup-validation` after architecture is drafted

check.if: a critical ambiguity blocks safe setup
check.ask: Ask one targeted question only, include recommended default, and explain what changes with each choice

step.goal: Finish with clear handoff to next-step
step.output: Initialization complete. Workspace is prepared for `/bmad:td:next-step`.
step.action: Report project mode detected, artifacts created/updated, td dependency mapping status, validation methodology status, and first recommended next-step action
Include explicit evidence in the report: created/verified BMAD workflows and resulting td epic/task counts
